qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Protocol-relative URL bypass in api_base_url OAuth client helpers leaks credentials
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a protocol-relative URL confinement bug in authlib's OAuth client request helpers, while the advisory is a JOSE/JWT signature-verification flaw where the default authlib.jose.jwt decoder accepted alg:none tokens with empty signatures. They do not share the vulnerable function, verification path, or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's JWT alg:none signature-verification flaw. The session's only qualifying finding concerns outbound OAuth client URL resolution and bearer-token forwarding, which is a different subsystem and root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes forged JWT acceptance when authlib.jose.jwt.decode(...) is used with alg:none and a blank signature.
Introducing commit a61c2acb807496e67f32051b5f1b1d5ccf8f0a75 changed NoneAlgorithm.verify to accept an empty signature, enabling unsecured JWS support.
Fix commit b87c32ed07b8ae7f805873e1c9cafd1016761df7 removed none from the default authlib.jose.jwt algorithm set and converted legitimate none cases to explicit opt-in JsonWebToken(["none"]).
The only final qualifying finding targets authlib.integrations.base_client api_base_url request helpers and urljoin-based protocol-relative host escape, not JOSE/JWT signature verification.
